title: Fix VPI callback list iteration
body:
Actually fix #2536. This is a follow-on to #2588 with a test.

Saving the **end()** iterator isn't enough, as it points to a non-existent element after the last element in the list.
Callbacks added with **push_back()** are inserted before it, so the same problem still exists.

We need to get the last element in the list when the loop starts, and exit once it's been processed.
I played with keeping an exit condition in the for loop, but I couldn't solve a segfault I was getting when dealing with the last element being erased.

If you have suggestions on a different approach, they are welcome.

In the test, I needed the list to have at least 2 callbacks at the start of the loop to get the error condition on master (thus the filler callback). With only 1 callback, the `it++` set it to the end iterator before a new callback was added and the loop was exited.
